role.setup_intro: Define roles with privileges per app function. Assign roles to devices per group in Connections.
role.function_access_hint: Enable each function and set what this role may do there (show, edit, or owner).
role.group_assignment_range_hint: When a device is assigned this role on a group, how far the assignment applies in the group tree.
role.group_access_hint: Enable a group, then choose how far access extends (range) and what the role may do (privilege).
role.no_roles_defined: No roles yet. Create one to begin.
role.no_groups: No groups in this community. Create groups first.
role.select_or_create_role: Select a role or create a new one.
role.name_required: Enter a role name before saving
role.name: Role name
role.inherited_no_disconnect: Change assignment on the parent group that set this role
role.no_role_profiles: No roles defined yet. Create roles under Connections → Roles & privileges.
role.no_roles_apply_to_group: No roles with function privileges defined. Open Roles & privileges and enable at least one function per role.
role.no_functions_enabled: No functions enabled for this role
role.unassigned_devices: Devices without a role on this group
role.local_creator_short: Creator on this device — full access until you pick a role
role.remote_no_access_hint: Paired devices with no role here cannot see this group until you assign one.
role.default_full_access_hint: These devices have no role assigned on this group — they keep full access until you restrict them.
role.select_group_first: Select a group to see its roles
role.no_roles: No roles yet for this group
role.creator_default_desc: No explicit roles — group owner has full access by default
role.sync_intro_reduction: The following role changes reduce access privileges. Applying this requires double confirmation.
role.sync_intro_extension: The following role changes extend access privileges.
role.sync_confirm_step1: I understand this will restrict access for the affected roles
role.sync_confirm_step2: Confirm and apply the privilege reduction
sync.connections_section_intro: After you assign privileges, confirm the sync rules. Data sync runs on the interval below once both devices have accepted the contract. Transport over LAN is planned in a later step.
sync.renew_contract_hint: Send the current role assignments to connected devices again (same flow as confirming changes).
sync.renew_no_remotes: Add at least one connected device before renewing the contract.
sync.contract_active: Sync contract is active on this device.
sync.contract_pending: No active contract yet — assign privileges and confirm when prompted.
sync.contract_confirm_intro: You changed device or role assignments. Before automatic sync can run, confirm that the privilege rules below are correct.
sync.contract_confirm_interval_hint: After both devices accept, sync will run every {seconds} seconds (configurable in Connections).
sync.confirm_pending_banner: There are unapproved changes to group access. Click the button to review data for acceptance:
sync.contract_confirm_checkbox: I confirm these privilege rules are correct for connected devices
sync.contract_preview_intro_first: This is the first sync contract with your connected devices. Review groups, roles, and what the other device can access.
sync.contract_preview_intro_update: Privilege or assignment changes will be sent to connected devices. Review the impact before sending.
sync.contract_preview_no_groups: No groups in this contract yet.
sync.contract_preview_no_roles: No role profiles with enabled functions.
sync.contract_preview_no_shared: No explicit role assignments to share — creator access stays on this device only.
sync.contract_preview_creator_note: Only roles you assign are shared. Creator access stays on this device.
sync.preview_inherited_scope: Scope includes all current and future subgroups within «{group}».
sync.contract_preview_incoming_note: Review the terms proposed by the other device. Creator access on your device is never shared.
sync.contract_reject_notify_fail: Could not notify the other device — check LAN connection.
sync.incoming_banner: «{device}» proposed sync changes — tap to review and accept.
sync.contract_revoked_banner: «{device}» ended the sync contract — send a new one to sync again.
sync.contract_remove_confirm: Remove the sync contract with «{device}»? The other device cannot sync until a new contract is sent.
sync.contract_revoked_local: Sync contract removed on this device.
sync.pending_send_banner: Contract is waiting to be delivered or accepted on the other device.
sync.pending_action_cancelled: Cancelled — you can confirm changes again in Connections
sync.missing_lan_host: No LAN address for: {names} — pair again via Wi‑Fi / LAN.
sync.lan_listen_required_receive: Turn on LAN listening on this device (Connections → Wi‑Fi / LAN) so the other device can receive contracts.
sync.lan_delivery_failed: Could not deliver the contract — on the other device run CO21, enable LAN listening, and verify the address in Connections (port 47321).
sync.contract_peer_intro: The other device must accept the same group sync rules. Until it does, periodic sync will not start.
sync.contract_peer_note: Higher privileges are not always better — e.g. an info screen may need only limited task preview. Reject or accept on the receiving device.
sync.contract_peer_accept_checkbox: I accept these sync rules on this device
sync.contract_signed_ok: Sync contract saved on this device
sync.contract_extension_note: Wider access may not suit every device (e.g. wall displays). Accept only if the remote device should receive this level.
sync.contract_tasks_access: Tasks in «{group}»: {priv} — {count} task(s) visible on the other device
sync.contract_tasks_access_empty: Tasks in «{group}»: {priv} — no tasks in this group yet
connections.internet_coming_soon: Internet server pairing is not available yet.
connections.bluetooth_disabled: Bluetooth pairing is not enabled in this build.
connections.device_name_persisted: Stored on this device — not changed when you edit sync settings or paired devices.
connections.device_name_from_system: Filled from this computer’s name. Change it here if you want a different label on the network.
connections.device_name_required: Enter a name for this device, then Save.
connections.check_no_host: No LAN address — pair again via Wi‑Fi / LAN.
connections.check_wrong_device: Reachable, but another CO21 device answered at this address.
connections.check_id_repaired: Connection OK. Device id was updated to match the peer.
lan.accept_pairing: Accept pairing requests from the devices connected to the router (LAN / Wifi)
lan.bonjour_hint: Search for other CO21 computers on your Wi‑Fi (.local).
lan.pc_host_label: PC IP or hostname (with or without port)
lan.qr_share_hint: On the phone: Connections → Router/LAN/Wifi → scan this QR, then Request pairing.
lan.enable_pairing_for_qr: Turn on “Accept pairing” to show the QR code and addresses.
lan.help.title: LAN pairing — connection help
lan.help.intro: If pairing times out or works only one way, use the sections below. CO21 uses TCP port {port} on your local network.
lan.help.probe_label: Quick test URL (open on the phone browser, Wi‑Fi only)
lan.help.inline_hint: Connection timed out. Tap “Connection help” for firewall and network steps (port {port}).
lan.help.body_quick_test:
On the phone (Wi‑Fi only — turn off mobile data), open in the browser:

http://{host}:{port}/co21-lan/v1/info

Success: JSON with deviceId, deviceName, appVersion.
Timeout: network or firewall — try the other sections.
lan.help.section_one_way: Works one way only (PC→phone OK, phone→PC fails)
lan.help.body_one_way:
This usually means the PC is blocking inbound connections on port {port}. Outbound from the PC is allowed; incoming from the phone is not.

Fix: allow inbound TCP {port} on the PC (see Linux or Windows below).
lan.help.body_linux:
If pairing works one way only, Linux is often blocking inbound TCP {port}.

firewalld:
  sudo firewall-cmd --list-all
  sudo firewall-cmd --add-port={port}/tcp --permanent
  sudo firewall-cmd --add-port=5353/udp --permanent
  sudo firewall-cmd --reload

ufw:
  sudo ufw status
  sudo ufw allow {port}/tcp
  sudo ufw allow 5353/udp

Quick test (until reboot):
  sudo iptables -I INPUT -p tcp --dport {port} -j ACCEPT

While pairing from the phone:
  sudo tcpdump -i <interface> -n port {port}

SYN packets from the phone but still timeout → firewall was blocking.
No packets → check router / Wi‑Fi isolation.
lan.help.body_windows:
CO21 desktop app must be running with “Accept pairing” ON.

Windows Defender Firewall:
  Set network profile to Private (not Public).
  Allow CO21 or inbound TCP port {port} for private networks.

Find PC IP: ipconfig → Ethernet or Wi‑Fi IPv4 (e.g. 192.168.x.x).
lan.help.body_router:
Phone and PC must be on the same subnet (e.g. both 192.168.0.x).

Turn off mobile data on the phone while pairing.

Guest Wi‑Fi and AP / client isolation block LAN pairing — use the main Wi‑Fi network.

PC on ethernet + phone on Wi‑Fi: some routers block Wi‑Fi→wired; disable AP isolation in router settings.

Disable phone VPN or Private DNS temporarily if pairing fails.
welcome.title: Welcome to CO21
welcome.subtitle_choose: How would you like to get started?
welcome.subtitle_new: Create your first group or a dedicated workspace folder.
welcome.subtitle_existing: Reconnect to an existing workspace, devices, or backup.
welcome.choice_new: I'm new — create my first group or workspace
welcome.choice_existing: I already have a workspace or backup
welcome.missing_workspace: The active workspace «{name}» folder was not found at {path}. It may have been moved or deleted.
space.title: Spaces
space.subtitle: Switch data folder and isolated settings. Changing space restarts the app.
space.desktop_only: Space management is available in the desktop app only.
space.path_hint: Groups, devices, and settings for this space are stored here.
space.create_mode_blank_hint: Create only CO21 project files — same as today.
space.create_mode_folder_manager_hint: Pick a folder with files (gallery or any files). CO21 creates a ~co21-workspace subfolder and a task to browse that folder.
space.create_mode_many_containers_hint: Pick a root folder. For each immediate subfolder, CO21 creates a separate browse or gallery task.
space.content_root_hint: Folder with your files. CO21 project data goes into a ~co21-workspace subfolder inside it.
space.workspace_path_hint: Created automatically inside the content folder.
space.created_with_tasks_ok: Workspace created with file tasks — switching now.
space.system_user_desc: Uses the default application folder for the signed-in Windows/macOS user: {path}
space.default_space_hint: Choose which space opens when you start the app. If none is selected, the last accessed space is used.
space.default_space_cleared: Default cleared — last accessed space will be used on start
space.open_services_hint: Open integrated services for this space — access, logging, and sign-in options
space.mode_files_desc: JSON files in the folder — easy to inspect, edit, back up, and share.
space.mode_sqlite_desc: Single co21.db — harder to read or share; original files are kept after migration.
space.migrate_first: Migrate to SQLite first. Original files are not removed.
space.migrate_ok: Migrated {groups} group(s) and {settings} setting key(s). Restarting…
space.sqlite_migrated: SQLite copy created on {date} (JSON files kept)
space.switch_confirm_body: The app will restart and load a separate set of groups, connections, and settings.
space.locate_existing_hint: Point to a folder that already contains CO21 data (groups, settings, or co21.db).
space.locate_path_hint: Select the workspace root folder on disk.
space.relocate_hint: Choose the new folder where this workspace was moved.
space.missing_dialog_intro_missing: CO21 couldn't find the project at the last location set for workspace «{name}».
space.missing_dialog_intro_no_data: CO21 couldn't find project data at the last location set for workspace «{name}».
space.missing_dialog_intro_moved: If the content was moved or a folder was renamed, you can choose another destination now or later.
space.missing_dialog_intro_drive: Check that the disk drive is connected to your device, then retry.
space.missing_dialog_help_prompt: If the problem is more serious, see possible solutions
space.missing_help_1: Reconnect the drive or network folder, then press Retry.
space.missing_help_2: Use Locate workspace if you moved or renamed the project folder.
space.missing_help_3: Restore from a backup: after switching to another workspace, open Menu → Connections → Backup and import a backup file.
space.missing_help_4: If the folder was deleted without a backup, look for a copy on another device or in cloud storage.
space.missing_default_broken: This workspace is set to open on start.
space.missing_locate_hint: Browse to where this workspace lives now (same data, new path).
space.missing_skip_hint: Continue without loading this workspace. Your other workspaces are unchanged.
space.missing_remove_hint: If you removed this project on purpose, delist it in CO21 or delete the project folder from disk.
space.remove_confirm_intro: Choose how CO21 should handle this workspace.
space.remove_option_delist_note: The project disappears from the list, but the user can point to its location again and add it back.
space.remove_option_delete_folder_note: Removed together with everything inside it. Files linked in the project but stored outside the project folder will not be deleted.
space.issues_banner: {count} workspace(s) need attention — locate the folder or move data.
space.path_no_data_caption: Folder exists but has no CO21 data: {path}
space.default_broken_blocked: Cannot set default — fix or locate this workspace first.
space.switch_broken_blocked: Cannot switch — locate this workspace folder first.
space.move_hint: Copy all workspace files to a new empty folder, update the path, then delete the old folder.
space.move_dest_hint: Choose an empty folder (or one that will be created).
space.move_confirm_body: All files will be copied to the new location. The old folder will be deleted afterwards.
space.move_confirm_warning: This cannot be undone. Close other programs using these files before continuing.
space.access.title: Space access
space.access.subtitle: Password for the active space only. Stored in this space folder; not synced to other devices.
space.access.lock_hint: Enter the password for this space to continue.
space.access.device_local_hint: Stored under this space folder in a device-specific subfolder. Other devices and LAN sync do not use your password file.

infoscreen.title: Screensaver, presentation, screen lock
infoscreen.variant_hint_layout_drift: Keeps the normal layout locked. The view drifts by a few pixels on a slow timer to reduce static burn-in.
infoscreen.variant_hint_wall_clock: Keeps the normal layout. Touch to use the app for 1 minute; optional lock when idle. Shows a large clock on a black screen at set intervals.
infoscreen.lock_screen_hint: When enabled, the screen is read-only until you touch it (then 1 minute of interaction).
infoscreen.clock_display_seconds_hint: How long the full-screen clock stays visible each time it appears (3–300 seconds).
accounts.title: Services and plugins
accounts.plugins_desc: Show optional plugin panels and extensions in the app. Off by default.
accounts.password_lock_hint: Locks the active workspace when the app starts.
accounts.feature_auth_desc: Use your Google account to sign in to the app
accounts.feature_calendar_desc: Synchronise tasks and events with Google Calendar
accounts.feature_drive_desc: Cloud backup and sharing — coming soon
accounts.google_connect_info: Connect your Google account to unlock additional features such as sign-in and calendar synchronisation.
accounts.disconnect_confirm_body: All Google features will be disabled. Your CO21 account and data stay untouched.
accounts.select_group_first: Select a group first to enable sync
accounts.nager_holidays_desc: Download public holidays from {api} for country {country}. Data is cached locally on your device.
accounts.meteo_desc: Show weather from {api} using device location or a country default. Data is cached locally.
accounts.clock_desc: Show the current time and date on the day organiser.
